scaled_oracle_cases.description: Combined test covering whitespace variations, empty lines, comments, and chromosome normalization.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify the function correctly parses input with whitespace variations (tabs, multiple spaces, leading/trailing spaces), skips empty lines and comments, and normalizes chromosome identifiers by stripping common prefixes.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must parse lines with any whitespace delimiters, ignore lines that are empty or start with '#', and return chromosome column values without 'chr' or 'chrom' prefix.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: A solution that splits on single spaces, does not skip comments/empty lines, or does not normalize chromosomes will produce incorrect output or errors.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
with open('combined.txt', 'w') as f:
    f.write('# This is a comment\n')
    f.write('\n')
    f.write('chr1:100-200\tgeneA\n')
    f.write('chrom2:300-400   \n')
    f.write('  chr3:500-600 REF>ALT\n')
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a file with whitespace variations and verify correct parsing.
Scaled executable cases must include an input file with comments and empty lines, verifying correct skipping.
Scaled executable cases must test that chromosome identifiers are normalized.
Scaled executable cases must include a file with comment lines and verify they are ignored.
